seasons.tagline: Pope Year 2. Oweh emerges as a star, freshmen Aberdeen and Moreno anchor the lineup, and Kentucky fights through injuries all season.
seasons.overview: Mark Pope's second season was defined by resilience. Jaland Lowe and Jayden Quaintance — two of the team's most talented players — missed significant time with injuries, forcing Pope to lean on a core of Otega Oweh (18.2 ppg), freshman Denzel Aberdeen (13.2 ppg), and sophomore center Malachi Moreno (8.0 ppg, 6.4 rpg, 1.6 bpg). Collin Chandler shot 42.7% from three and provided steady perimeter scoring. The Wildcats finished 21-13 in a brutally competitive SEC.

seasons.tagline: Year One of the Pope era. A transfer-heavy roster, key wins over Gonzaga, Duke, Louisville, and Florida, and a Sweet Sixteen run that announced Kentucky was back.
seasons.overview: Mark Pope's first season exceeded expectations. Built almost entirely from the transfer portal after John Calipari's departure to Arkansas, the Wildcats went 24-12 with signature wins over Gonzaga, Duke in the Champions Classic, and rivals Louisville, Florida, and Tennessee. As a #3 seed, Kentucky beat Troy and Illinois before falling to Tennessee in the Sweet Sixteen. Otega Oweh led the team in scoring as the transfer from Oklahoma became the face of the Pope era's first chapter.

seasons.accTournament: SEC Tournament (Lost in QF to Texas A&M)
seasons.tagline: Calipari's last season. Antonio Reeves' 20-point scoring. Reed Sheppard's record-breaking efficiency. Rob Dillingham's instant-offense magic. And an Oakland-shaped ending nobody saw coming.
seasons.overview: John Calipari's 15th and final season at Kentucky was a study in extremes. Antonio Reeves scored 20.2 points per game with elite three-point shooting. Reed Sheppard put up one of the most efficient freshman stat lines in program history — 52.1% from three, 2.5 steals per game — and was named SEC Freshman of the Year. Rob Dillingham provided instant offense off the bench. But the defense was historically bad for a Calipari team, and it caught up with Kentucky in the NCAA Tournament when Oakland's upset in the first round ended the season and, effectively, the Calipari era.

seasons.accTournament: SEC Tournament (Lost in QF to Vanderbilt)
seasons.tagline: Oscar Tshiebwe's encore season. Antonio Reeves' breakout. Cason Wallace's freshman brilliance. And a second-round exit that left Kentucky wanting more.
seasons.overview: Oscar Tshiebwe returned as the reigning National Player of the Year and averaged another double-double (16.4 ppg, 13.1 rpg), but missed time with a knee procedure. Antonio Reeves transferred in from Illinois State and scored 14.2 per game, earning SEC Co-Sixth Man of the Year honors. Freshman Cason Wallace ran the point with poise (11.7 ppg, 4.2 apg, 1.9 spg). Kentucky earned a #6 seed but fell to Kansas State in the second round.

seasons.tagline: Oscar Tshiebwe's national player of the year season. TyTy Washington's brilliance. And the most shocking first-round upset in tournament history.
seasons.overview: Oscar Tshiebwe transferred from West Virginia and put together one of the greatest individual seasons in Kentucky history — 17.4 ppg, 15.1 rpg, consensus National Player of the Year. TyTy Washington ran the point as a freshman (12.5 ppg). Kellan Grady transferred in and shot 41.5% from three. Kentucky earned a #2 seed but was stunned by 15-seed Saint Peter's in one of the most iconic upsets in NCAA Tournament history.

seasons.tagline: The 9-16 season. COVID, injuries, Terrence Clarke's tragic death, and the worst record in modern Kentucky history.
seasons.overview: The most painful season in modern Kentucky history. Projected as a top-10 team, the Wildcats went 9-16 — the program's worst record since 1926-27. Brandon Boston and Davion Mintz led the scoring, but nothing clicked. Terrence Clarke, who missed most of the season with a leg injury, was tragically killed in a car accident in April 2021 after declaring for the NBA Draft. The season was a low point, but it set the stage for Oscar Tshiebwe's transfer and the program's rebound.

seasons.tagline: Immanuel Quickley's breakout. Nick Richards' transformation. Tyrese Maxey's one-and-done brilliance. And the season COVID stole.
seasons.overview: Kentucky was 25-6, ranked #8, and on track for a #2 or #3 seed when the NCAA Tournament was cancelled due to COVID-19. Immanuel Quickley won SEC Player of the Year after transforming from a role player into a 16.1 ppg scorer who shot 92.3% from the free throw line. Nick Richards became a dominant center (14.0 ppg, 7.8 rpg, 2.1 bpg). Tyrese Maxey was electric as a freshman (13.9 ppg). It remains one of the great what-if seasons in Kentucky history.

seasons.tagline: PJ Washington's breakout. Tyler Herro's clutch shooting. The Elite Eight run that ended in Auburn heartbreak.
seasons.overview: PJ Washington returned for his sophomore season and became the team's best player (15.2 ppg, 7.6 rpg). Tyler Herro was electric as a freshman (14.0 ppg, 93.5% FT). Keldon Johnson provided another scoring punch (13.5 ppg). Ashton Hagans ran the point with 160 assists. Kentucky reached the Elite Eight as a #2 seed before Auburn's controversial no-call in overtime ended the season.

seasons.tagline: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander's breakout. Kevin Knox's scoring. PJ Washington's arrival. The Sweet Sixteen run that built the 2019 team.
seasons.overview: Shai Gilgeous-Alexander emerged as a star, averaging 14.4 ppg and 5.1 apg as a freshman — numbers that would make him the 11th pick and, eventually, an MVP candidate. Kevin Knox led the team in scoring (15.6 ppg). PJ Washington showed flashes of what was to come (10.8 ppg). Hamidou Diallo was electric athletically (10.0 ppg). Kentucky reached the Sweet Sixteen as a #5 seed before falling to Kansas State.

seasons.tagline: Malik Monk's 47-point explosion. De'Aaron Fox burying Lonzo Ball. Bam Adebayo's dominance inside. The Elite Eight heartbreak against UNC.
seasons.overview: Malik Monk scored 19.8 ppg with a 47-point game against North Carolina in February. De'Aaron Fox ran the show (16.7 ppg, 4.6 apg) and delivered a legendary 39-point performance to eliminate Lonzo Ball's UCLA in the Sweet Sixteen. Bam Adebayo anchored the frontcourt (13.0 ppg, 8.0 rpg). Isaiah Briscoe was the glue guy (12.1 ppg, 5.4 rpg, 4.2 apg). Kentucky reached the Elite Eight as a #2 seed before falling to North Carolina 75-73 on a Luke Maye buzzer-beater.

seasons.tagline: Jamal Murray and Tyler Ulis formed the most electric backcourt in the country, but a second-round exit left the season feeling incomplete.
seasons.overview: The 2015-16 Wildcats were powered by freshman Jamal Murray (20.0 ppg) and sophomore Tyler Ulis, who won SEC Player of the Year and Consensus All-American honors. Ulis ran the show with 7.0 assists per game while Murray provided scoring firepower from everywhere on the floor. Despite a strong SEC regular season, Kentucky was upset by Indiana in the second round of the NCAA Tournament.

seasons.tagline: 38-0. The perfect season that wasn't. Karl-Anthony Towns, Devin Booker, and the heartbreak in Indianapolis.
seasons.overview: Kentucky entered the 2015 NCAA Tournament as the first undefeated team in decades, riding a 38-0 record. The dream ended in the Final Four against Wisconsin.

seasons.tagline: Julius Randle, the Harrison twins, and a freshman-laden squad that danced all the way to the title game before falling to UConn.
seasons.overview: Kentucky's 2013-14 team was built around a historically talented freshman class headlined by Julius Randle, James Young, and the Harrison twins. After a rocky regular season that included an NIT-caliber stretch in January, the Wildcats caught fire in March. Aaron Harrison hit clutch three-pointers in three consecutive tournament games to carry Kentucky to the national championship game, where they fell 60-54 to Connecticut.

seasons.tagline: The hangover season. Nerlens Noel tore his ACL, the Wildcats missed the NCAA Tournament, and landed in the NIT.
seasons.overview: Coming off the national championship, Kentucky's 2012-13 season was defined by adversity. Nerlens Noel was the centerpiece — averaging 10.5 points, 9.5 rebounds, and 4.4 blocks — but tore his ACL in February against Florida. Without him, the Wildcats stumbled to a 21-12 record and missed the NCAA Tournament for the first time under Calipari, losing in the first round of the NIT to Robert Morris.

seasons.tagline: The most dominant college basketball team in a generation. Anthony Davis, MKG, and the 2012 national championship.
seasons.overview: Led by Anthony Davis — the consensus national player of the year — Kentucky went 38-2, ran through the SEC undefeated, and beat Kansas 67-59 for the program's eighth national championship.

seasons.tagline: Calipari's second team. Brandon Knight, Terrence Jones, and a Final Four run that announced Kentucky was back.
seasons.overview: John Calipari's second Kentucky team featured freshman Brandon Knight, who averaged 17.3 points per game and hit the shot that beat Ohio State in the Sweet 16. Terrence Jones led the team in rebounds with 8.8 per game. Josh Harrellson emerged as a fan favorite and rebounding force. The Wildcats reached the Final Four before falling to Connecticut, who went on to win the national championship.

seasons.tagline: Year One of the Calipari era. John Wall, DeMarcus Cousins, and the announcement that Kentucky was back.
seasons.overview: John Calipari's arrival in Lexington brought an immediate turnaround. John Wall and DeMarcus Cousins led the Wildcats to a 35-3 record and the Elite Eight.

seasons.tagline: Jodie Meeks scored 54 against Tennessee. Patterson returned. Gillispie was fired. The season ended in the NIT.
seasons.overview: The 2008-09 season was defined by extremes. Jodie Meeks erupted for 54 points against Tennessee — the most by any SEC player in a conference game — and Patrick Patterson continued his development into an All-SEC force. But Gillispie lost the locker room, the Wildcats stumbled to 22-14, and missed the NCAA Tournament. Gillispie was fired after the season and replaced by John Calipari.

seasons.tagline: Billy Gillispie's first season. Joe Crawford, Patrick Patterson, and a first-round exit.
seasons.overview: Billy Gillispie arrived from Texas A&M to replace Tubby Smith and inherited a roster led by seniors Joe Crawford and Ramel Bradley. Freshman Patrick Patterson showed immediate star potential, averaging 16.4 points and 7.7 rebounds. Jodie Meeks was limited to 11 games with a hip injury. The Wildcats went 18-13 and lost to Marquette in the first round of the NCAA Tournament.

seasons.tagline: Tubby Smith's final season. Randolph Morris, Joe Crawford, and Ramel Bradley led a second-round exit before Smith departed for Minnesota.
seasons.overview: The 2006-07 season was Tubby Smith's last in Lexington. Randolph Morris led the team at 16.1 points and 7.8 rebounds. Joe Crawford and Ramel Bradley formed a potent backcourt. Freshman Jodie Meeks showed flashes of the scorer he'd become, averaging 8.7 points. Kentucky went 22-12 and lost to Kansas in the second round of the NCAA Tournament. Smith resigned shortly after to take the Minnesota job, ending a 10-year run that included a national championship in 1998.

seasons.tagline: Rondo's breakout. Randolph Morris returned midseason. A second-round exit ended Tubby's penultimate year.
seasons.overview: Rajon Rondo took over as the engine of the 2005-06 team, averaging 11.2 points, 6.1 rebounds, and 4.9 assists. Randolph Morris missed the first half of the season due to an NCAA suspension but returned to average 13.3 points in 21 games. Patrick Sparks provided veteran scoring. The Wildcats went 22-13 and fell in the second round of the NCAA Tournament to Connecticut.

seasons.tagline: Tubby's last Elite Eight. Chuck Hayes, Kelenna Azubuike, a freshman Rajon Rondo, and Patrick Sparks' miracle three against Michigan State.
seasons.overview: The 2004-05 Wildcats were led by senior Chuck Hayes, who won SEC Defensive Player of the Year, and junior Kelenna Azubuike, who averaged 14.7 points. Freshman Rajon Rondo made an immediate impact with 87 steals and 118 assists. Patrick Sparks hit a legendary contested three-pointer from NBA range to force overtime against Michigan State in a double-overtime Elite Eight classic. Kentucky fell just short, losing 94-88 in the regional final.

seasons.tagline: Gerald Fitch and Erik Daniels led a 27-win squad that swept the SEC Tournament, then were shocked by UAB in the second round of the NCAAs.
seasons.overview: The 2003-04 Wildcats finished 27-5 and earned a #1 seed in the NCAA Tournament behind seniors Gerald Fitch and Erik Daniels, who both earned All-SEC First Team honors. Chuck Hayes emerged as a dominant interior force, averaging 10.7 points and 8.1 rebounds. Cliff Hawkins ran the point with 166 assists. Kentucky won the SEC East and swept through the SEC Tournament, beating Georgia, South Carolina, and Florida. The season ended in heartbreak when #14-seed UAB stunned Kentucky 76-75 in the second round in Columbus.

seasons.tagline: A 32-win juggernaut that went 16-0 in the SEC. Keith Bogans, Marquis Estill, and Tubby Smith's National Coach of the Year campaign ended in the Elite Eight against Dwyane Wade's Marquette.
seasons.overview: The 2002-03 Wildcats posted a dominant 32-4 record and went undefeated in SEC play at 16-0, finishing #1 in the AP poll. Senior Keith Bogans earned SEC Player of the Year and third-team All-American honors, averaging 15.7 points. Marquis Estill anchored the interior with 11.6 points, 6.0 rebounds, and 1.8 blocks. Chuck Hayes contributed 8.6 points and a team-high 6.8 rebounds as a sophomore. Tubby Smith swept every major National Coach of the Year award. Kentucky won the SEC regular season and tournament titles before advancing to the Elite Eight as a #1 seed, where Dwyane Wade and Marquette ended the season 83-69.

seasons.tagline: "Team Turmoil" — Tayshaun Prince's 41 against Tulsa, a classic OT loss to #1 Duke, and a Sweet Sixteen exit to eventual champion Maryland.
seasons.overview: The 2001-02 Wildcats finished 22-10 in a turbulent season. Tayshaun Prince earned third-team All-American honors, averaging 17.5 points and 6.3 rebounds. Keith Bogans added 11.6 points per game. The season was marred by Jason Parker's ACL injury, Marvin Stone's mid-season departure to Louisville, and recurring discipline issues. Despite losing in the first round of the SEC Tournament to South Carolina, Kentucky earned a #4 seed in the NCAAs. Prince scored 41 points against Tulsa in the second round before the run ended against Maryland in the Sweet Sixteen.

seasons.tagline: Tayshaun Prince won SEC Player of the Year and Saul Smith ran the point in his father's system. SEC co-champs and tourney champs, but fell to USC in the Sweet Sixteen.
seasons.overview: The 2000-01 Wildcats went 24-10 behind consensus second-team All-American Tayshaun Prince, who averaged 16.9 points and 6.5 rebounds while winning SEC Player of the Year. Keith Bogans led the team at 17.0 points per game as a sophomore. Saul Smith captained the team and dished 135 assists. Jason Parker was the SEC Freshman of the Year with 8.6 points and 4.7 rebounds. Kentucky shared the SEC regular season title with Florida and won the SEC Tournament. As a #2 seed, the Wildcats beat Holy Cross and Iowa before falling to USC 80-76 in the Sweet Sixteen.

seasons.tagline: Jamaal Magloire's senior year, freshman Keith Bogans arrived, and Tayshaun Prince emerged. SEC co-champs but a second-round NCAA exit to Syracuse.
seasons.overview: The 1999-00 Wildcats went 23-10 behind senior Jamaal Magloire, who earned All-SEC First Team with 13.2 points, 9.1 rebounds, and 1.7 blocks. Sophomore Tayshaun Prince led scoring at 13.3 points. Freshman Keith Bogans showed promise with 12.5 points per game. Kentucky shared the SEC regular season title and reached the NCAAs as a #5 seed but fell to Syracuse 52-50 in the second round.

seasons.tagline: Defending champs returned without Sheppard, Mohammed, Edwards, and Mills. Padgett, Turner, and Evans led a 28-win squad to another Elite Eight before falling to Michigan State.
seasons.overview: The 1998-99 Wildcats went 28-9 defending their national title. Scott Padgett led with 12.6 points and was named SEC Tournament MVP. Heshimu Evans contributed 11.8 points and 5.1 rebounds, while Wayne Turner ran the offense with 145 assists. Freshman Tayshaun Prince showed flashes with 5.8 points per game. Kentucky won the SEC Tournament, beating Mississippi, Auburn, and Arkansas. As a #3 seed, they beat New Mexico State, Kansas in overtime, and Miami (OH) before falling to Michigan State 73-66 in the Elite Eight.

seasons.tagline: Tubby Smith's first season, Kentucky's eighth national championship. Jeff Sheppard was Final Four MOP, and the Cats beat Duke 86-84 in the regional final before topping Utah for the title.
seasons.overview: In Tubby Smith's debut season, the Wildcats went 35-4 and won the national championship. Jeff Sheppard earned Final Four Most Outstanding Player, averaging 13.7 points. Nazr Mohammed dominated inside with 12.0 points and 7.2 rebounds. Scott Padgett contributed 11.5 points and 6.5 rebounds. Wayne Turner ran the floor with 173 assists and was named Regional MOP. Kentucky beat Duke 86-84 in the Elite Eight, edged Stanford 86-85 in overtime in the semis, and defeated Utah 78-69 for the title in San Antonio.

seasons.tagline: Pitino's last ride. Ron Mercer was consensus All-American. Derek Anderson tore his ACL mid-season, but UK still reached the title game before falling to Arizona in overtime.
seasons.overview: The 1996-97 Wildcats went 35-5 in Rick Pitino's final season. Ron Mercer earned consensus first-team All-American and SEC Player of the Year honors with 18.1 points. Derek Anderson averaged 17.7 before a devastating ACL tear in January. Anthony Epps distributed 193 assists from the point. Nazr Mohammed and Jamaal Magloire anchored the post. Kentucky won the SEC Tournament and advanced to the national championship game as a #1 seed, beating Utah in the Elite Eight and Minnesota in the semis before losing to Arizona 84-79 in overtime.

seasons.tagline: The Untouchables. Tony Delk hit seven threes in the championship game. 34-2, 16-0 in the SEC. Kentucky's sixth national title.
seasons.overview: The 1995-96 Wildcats went 34-2 and won Kentucky's sixth national championship. Tony Delk earned Final Four MOP and SEC Player of the Year, averaging 17.8 points. Antoine Walker added 15.2 points and 8.4 rebounds as a sophomore. Walter McCarty contributed 11.3 points and 5.7 rebounds. Kentucky went 16-0 in the SEC, the first undefeated conference season since 1970. Delk hit seven three-pointers in the championship game as Kentucky beat Syracuse 76-67 in East Rutherford.

seasons.tagline: Tony Delk, Rodrick Rhodes, Walter McCarty, and freshman Antoine Walker. SEC champs and tourney champs, but fell to North Carolina in the Elite Eight.
seasons.overview: The 1994-95 Wildcats went 28-5 under Rick Pitino. Tony Delk led with 16.7 points per game, while Rodrick Rhodes added 12.9. Walter McCarty contributed 10.5 points and 5.6 rebounds. Freshman Antoine Walker made an immediate impact as SEC Tournament MVP with 7.8 points per game. Mark Pope anchored the interior with 8.2 points and 6.3 rebounds. Anthony Epps ran the point with 138 assists. Kentucky won the SEC regular season and tournament, then advanced to the Elite Eight as a #1 seed before falling to North Carolina 74-61.

seasons.tagline: Post-Mashburn transition year. Tony Delk and Travis Ford led the offense, Rodney Dent went down with injury, and Marquette upset Kentucky in the second round.
seasons.overview: The 1993-94 Wildcats went 27-7 in the first season after Jamal Mashburn's departure. Tony Delk stepped into the lead role with 16.6 points, while senior Travis Ford ran the show with 193 assists and was named SEC Tournament MVP. Rodney Dent averaged 10.5 points but was lost to injury after 11 games. Jared Prickett contributed 8.2 points and 7.0 rebounds. Andre Riddick blocked 83 shots. Kentucky won the SEC Tournament, beating #1 Arkansas along the way, but was upset by Marquette 75-63 in the second round of the NCAAs.

seasons.tagline: Jamal Mashburn's masterpiece — consensus All-American, SEC Player of the Year. Travis Ford shot 53% from three. A 30-win team that reached the Final Four before losing to Michigan's Fab Five in overtime.
seasons.overview: The 1992-93 Wildcats went 30-4 behind consensus first-team All-American Jamal Mashburn, who averaged 21.0 points, 8.4 rebounds, and 3.6 assists while earning SEC Player of the Year. Travis Ford was lethal from deep, shooting 52.9% from three with 101 makes. Dale Brown added 9.4 points in his senior year. Freshman Rodrick Rhodes contributed 9.1. Kentucky won the SEC Tournament and reached the Final Four as a #1 seed, dominating Wake Forest 103-69 and Florida State 106-81 in the regional, before falling to Michigan's Fab Five 81-78 in overtime in the national semifinal.

seasons.tagline: The Unforgettables. Pelphrey, Feldhaus, Farmer, and Woods took the program from probation to within one shot of the Final Four. Christian Laettner's buzzer-beater ended it 104-103 in overtime.
seasons.overview: The 1991-92 Wildcats went 29-7 in a magical season. Jamal Mashburn led with 21.3 points as a sophomore. The four senior captains — John Pelphrey, Deron Feldhaus, Richie Farmer, and Sean Woods — became known as 'The Unforgettables' for staying with the program through NCAA sanctions. Kentucky won the SEC Tournament and advanced to the Elite Eight as a #2 seed, where they lost to #1 Duke 104-103 in overtime on Christian Laettner's legendary turnaround jumper — widely considered the greatest game in NCAA Tournament history.

seasons.tagline: Still banned from the postseason, Pitino's Wildcats went 22-6 with the best record in the SEC. Freshman Mashburn arrived. Reggie Hanson's senior season. The program was back.
seasons.overview: In Pitino's second season, Kentucky went 22-6 despite remaining ineligible for postseason play due to NCAA probation. The Wildcats posted the SEC's best conference record at 14-4. Senior Reggie Hanson led with 14.4 points and 7.2 rebounds, while junior John Pelphrey matched him at 14.4 points. Freshman Jamal Mashburn arrived with 12.9 points and 7.0 rebounds per game. Deron Feldhaus (10.8) and Richie Farmer (10.1) gave Kentucky five double-figure scorers. Sean Woods dished 151 assists. Pitino won Sporting News National Coach of the Year and AP SEC Coach of the Year.

seasons.tagline: Pitino's first year. No scholarships, no postseason, no problem — he won National Coach of the Year anyway. The 150-95 loss at Kansas became the rallying cry. The rebuild had begun.
seasons.overview: Rick Pitino arrived at Kentucky in 1989 to a program devastated by NCAA sanctions. With limited scholarships and a postseason ban, the Wildcats went 14-14. Derrick Miller led with 19.2 points per game. Reggie Hanson added 16.4 points and 7.1 rebounds. The four future 'Unforgettables' — sophomores Pelphrey (13.0), Feldhaus (14.4), Farmer (7.0), and Woods (9.1) — formed the core. The 150-95 loss at Kansas became a galvanizing moment. Despite the record, Pitino won National Coach of the Year from Basketball Times and SEC Coach of the Year from UPI for the transformation he had begun.

seasons.tagline: The dark year. Sutton's final season amid the NCAA scandal. Chris Mills, LeRon Ellis, and Derrick Miller played hard, but the program crumbled to 13-19. Eric Manuel was banned. Rex Chapman had gone pro.
seasons.overview: Eddie Sutton's final season at Kentucky was his worst. The Wildcats went 13-19 amid a growing NCAA investigation that would eventually result in major sanctions. Rex Chapman had left for the NBA draft. Eric Manuel was declared ineligible. LeRon Ellis led with 16.0 points and 5.5 rebounds. Freshman Chris Mills added 14.3 points and 8.7 rebounds before the infamous Emery envelope scandal emerged. Derrick Miller contributed 13.9 points. Sean Sutton ran the point with 146 assists. Freshmen Deron Feldhaus, Richie Farmer, and John Pelphrey — future Unforgettables — began their careers in the wreckage.

seasons.tagline: Rex Chapman (19.0 ppg), Ed Davender, Winston Bennett — Kentucky was #1 in the country for much of the year. SEC Tourney champs, but some wins later vacated by NCAA investigation.
seasons.overview: The 1987-88 Wildcats went 25-5 (with some results later vacated by NCAA sanctions). Rex Chapman averaged 19.0 points as a sophomore and earned All-American recognition. Winston Bennett returned from his redshirt to average 15.3 points and 7.8 rebounds. Ed Davender added 15.7 points in his senior year. Kentucky reached #1 in the AP poll and won the SEC Tournament before falling to Villanova in the Sweet Sixteen. The NCAA investigation into the program was already underway.

seasons.tagline: Freshman Rex Chapman electrified Rupp Arena. Winston Bennett missed the season with a redshirt. An 18-11 team lost to Ohio State in the first round of the NCAAs.
seasons.overview: The 1986-87 season was a transition year after Kenny Walker's departure. Freshman Rex Chapman made an immediate impact with 16.0 points and All-SEC First Team honors. Ed Davender added 15.2 points and 101 assists. Richard Madison contributed 9.1 points and 7.4 rebounds. Winston Bennett sat out the season (redshirt after knee injury). Kentucky went 18-11 and earned an NCAA bid as an 8-seed but lost to Ohio State 91-77 in the first round.

seasons.tagline: Eddie Sutton's debut. Kenny Walker — consensus All-American, SEC Player of the Year, 20.0 ppg. 32-4, 17-1 SEC. Lost to LSU in the Elite Eight.
seasons.overview: Eddie Sutton's first season at Kentucky was a resounding success. The Wildcats went 32-4 and 17-1 in the SEC behind consensus first-team All-American Kenny Walker, who averaged 20.0 points and 7.7 rebounds. Winston Bennett added 12.7 points and 7.0 rebounds. Roger Harden dished 232 assists. Kentucky won the SEC regular season and tournament, then advanced to the Elite Eight as a #1 seed before falling to LSU 59-57 in Atlanta. Sutton won AP and NABC National Coach of the Year.
